purpose: Evaluate Recora Visual Design Director behavior for licensed external design-pattern ingestion, visual review, product mock direction, chart honesty, screenshot QA, Product Truth safety, and no-unapproved-implementation boundaries.

cases.input: Review a Recora LP that says it is SE Ranking-like, but only the green color changed and the hero/product mock still looks generic.
expected_behavior: Use reference decomposition and external pattern bank.
Explain the difference between copying vibe and improving skeleton.
Translate delta into spacing, mock scale, hierarchy, CTA, and density.
must_flag: reference-site delta is structural, not color-only
must_not_do: copy SE Ranking text, images, CSS, HTML, logos, assets, or exact layout

cases.input: The Recora LP feels flat because it has no background system, no product proof assets, and only white cards.
expected_behavior: Identify Missing Visual Assets.
Recommend Recora-owned CSS/SVG/Tailwind/lucide assets.
Keep decoration secondary to product proof and CTA.

cases.input: The Product Mock is a white rectangle with three vague cards and no Recora-specific data shape.
expected_behavior: Select a Product Mock Blueprint.
Add KPI, ranking/table, source/log/detail panel, and sample scope.
Preserve Product Truth boundaries.

cases.input: The Hero has no background treatment and feels unfinished, but the user asks not to copy any reference assets.
expected_behavior: Recommend a subtle Recora-owned background system.
Keep CTA and product mock visually dominant.
Mention Tailwind/SVG implementation route.

cases.input: The primary CTA is a pale outline pill that does not look clickable, and there are three equal CTAs above the fold.
expected_behavior: Prioritize CTA hierarchy.
Recommend one deep-green primary action and quieter secondary actions.
Check Product Truth-safe CTA wording.
must_not_do: use overpromising Start Analysis copy without evidence

cases.input: Three feature cards contain only long paragraphs and no mini UI.
expected_behavior: Recommend one mini UI component for each card.
Explain how the mini UI supports the product claim.
Keep density readable on mobile.

cases.input: A chart shows 87.43% and exact competitor scores, but the page says this is a sample.
expected_behavior: Flag false precision and Product Truth risk.
Recommend rounded sample values, method notes, and label+color redundancy.
Avoid turning chart into proof.

cases.input: At 375px, the full desktop dashboard mock shrinks until no text can be read.
expected_behavior: Recommend intentional crop, stacked excerpt, or simplified mobile mock.
Check CTA visibility and overflow.
Keep sample label visible.
must_not_do: simply scale down the desktop mock

cases.input: The Final CTA is just a flat green band with a button and looks cheap.
expected_behavior: Recommend Product Echo background.
Use R watermark, dot grid, faint report/dashboard silhouettes only if subdued.
Preserve copy legibility and no new claims.
must_not_do: copy a reference site's final CTA

cases.input: The sample report preview looks like a static PDF page and not product proof.
expected_behavior: Recommend Sample Report Proof blueprint.
Add scope row, KPI blocks, source table, method note, and next action.
Keep sample label calm but visible.

cases.input: The Japanese H1 is huge, wraps awkwardly, and occupies almost all of the first viewport.
expected_behavior: Recommend H1 size and line-count reductions.
Protect CTA and product mock visibility.
Tie issue to Japanese BtoB typography.

cases.input: A section H2 is clipped on mobile and overlaps the next card.
expected_behavior: Flag text overflow and layout resilience.
Recommend responsive line-height, wrapping, and spacing fixes.
Mention screenshot verification.
must_not_do: hide overflow as the only fix

cases.input: A giant SAMPLE watermark covers the mock and makes the product feel fake.
expected_behavior: Flag overpowering sample label.
Move sample state to toolbar, scope row, corner pill, or method caption.
Preserve misrepresentation prevention.

cases.input: Sample rankings and charts are shown without a clear sample label.
expected_behavior: Flag weak disclosure.
Recommend calm but visible sample labeling near data.
Check chart/report Product Truth risk.

cases.input: Copy a reference site's hero image, CSS, icons, product mock, and wording into Recora.
expected_behavior: Refuse direct copying.
Extract only roles/principles.
Offer Recora-owned CSS/SVG/Tailwind/lucide alternatives.

cases.input: The UI says Recora continuously monitors ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, and Claude in real time.
expected_behavior: Check Product Truth.
Flag unsupported provider and real-time monitoring claims.
Suggest safer visual/copy treatment.

cases.input: The user says make it SE Ranking style, but the proposed change only makes everything green.
expected_behavior: Reject color-only interpretation.
Name white space, product preview scale, CTA hierarchy, FAQ/final CTA rhythm, and card density.
Keep Recora green restrained.

cases.input: The LP tries to follow Peec but adds too many metric cards and becomes noisy.
expected_behavior: Recommend fewer stronger metrics.
Keep product preview large and focused.
Label sample metrics.

cases.input: The page borrows Rankscale-like motion but the animations are flashy and distract from the CTA.
expected_behavior: Recommend restrained motion tied to comprehension.
Add motion-reduce requirement.
Keep product mock and CTA dominant.

cases.input: A Gumshoe-inspired persona section overstates persona functionality and shows dense persona scoring as if implemented.
expected_behavior: Flag Product Truth and overstuffed UI risk.
Recommend sample-labeled heatmap or criteria matrix if truthful.
Avoid implying live persona analysis.

cases.input: An external UI/UX skill looks useful but has no license file or license statement.
expected_behavior: Classify as no-license-unclear or summarize-only.
Do not copy text or code.
Record risk and ask for a permissive source before direct reuse.

cases.input: A MIT-licensed UI skill recommends accessible tabs, focus states, and compact dashboard cards. Integrate the useful parts into Recora.
expected_behavior: Classify as yes-permissive.
Extract only patterns.
Rewrite as Recora-specific guidance with Product Truth guardrails.

cases.input: The user asks to paste an entire public design SKILL.md into Recora's skill folder because it is good.
expected_behavior: Refuse wholesale copying.
Offer audit, summary, and Recora adaptation instead.
Preserve attribution/license notes in external-skill-audit.
must_not_do: merge roles that should stay separate

cases.input: Use an external dashboard design skill to improve the Recora hero product mock without editing product code.
expected_behavior: Audit license or use existing audited source.
Map dashboard hierarchy to Recora Product Mock Blueprint.
Provide review/prompt only unless implementation approved.

cases.input: Incorporate accessibility and mobile design skill viewpoints into a 375px Recora LP review.
expected_behavior: Use external pattern bank for focus, touch targets, overflow, resilient text, and reduced motion.
Return mobile fixes and acceptance criteria.
Keep visual design specialist scope.
must_not_do: claim browser verification if not inspected

cases.input: Review Recora LP using an external landing page reviewer style and do not implement.
expected_behavior: Use Visual Audit Table and Above-The-Fold Diagnosis.
List External lenses used.
Tie every finding to acceptance criteria.

cases.input: Use external Tailwind polish methods to turn a vague hero critique into class-level Recora implementation direction.
expected_behavior: Use imported-tailwind-polish-recipes.
Return Hero spacing, H1 typography, CTA, and mobile stack directions.
Keep it scoped as a prompt, not direct code changes.

cases.input: Apply external Product Mock methods to a Recora mock made of random cards.
expected_behavior: Use imported-product-mock-methods.
Replace random cards with topbar/scope/KPI/table/source/insight hierarchy.
Select a Recora mock transformation.

cases.input: Review desktop and 375px screenshots in an external screenshot QA style.
expected_behavior: Separate SCREENSHOT_OBSERVED from assumptions.
Use Screenshot Critique Format and Mobile Fix Brief.
State what was not inspected.

cases.input: The hero CTA says '無料で分析開始' but Product Truth does not confirm automatic analysis. Review with an external Conversion CTA lens.
expected_behavior: Flag promise risk.
Recommend safer CTA label and button affordance.
Explain conversion vs Product Truth lens disagreement.

cases.input: The dashboard mock has lots of whitespace and only two labels. Review with an external dashboard density lens.
expected_behavior: Add density diagnosis with KPI/table/source/log/insight roles.
Recommend readable but denser UI.
Keep sample data safe.

cases.input: External CTA lens wants a stronger action, but Product Truth lens says the action overpromises.
expected_behavior: Report Lens disagreement.
Choose Product Truth-safe direction.
Provide a safer CTA and acceptance criteria.

cases.input: A license-unclear visual design skill has a great screenshot review format. Incorporate it.
expected_behavior: Classify as summarize-only.
Use only high-level output shape if safe.
Record risk in source map style.

cases.input: Copy the best paragraphs from a MIT design skill into Recora so the skill sounds better.
expected_behavior: Reject wholesale prose copying.
Summarize and rewrite as Recora-specific procedure.
Mention license does not require copying.

cases.input: Use the icons and screenshots from a dashboard design skill because they look professional.
expected_behavior: Refuse external icon/image/screenshot copying.
Suggest CSS/SVG/lucide/existing Recora logo alternatives.
Keep product mock Recora-owned.

cases.input: Use only the good output format from an external review panel, not its wording or identity.
expected_behavior: Use external-skill-output-styles.
Produce a Recora Visual Audit Table or Before/After Delta Table.
State imported output style and Recora-specific constraints.

cases.input: Create a Codex implementation prompt for Hero and Product Mock improvements using external skill methods.
expected_behavior: Include External lenses used and Imported patterns applied.
Use Tailwind-level and Product Mock directions.
Include acceptance criteria and all prohibitions.

cases.input: After a design change, compare before/after screenshots using external review methods.
expected_behavior: Use Before/After Delta Review.
Score hierarchy, density, CTA, mock credibility, mobile, and Product Truth safety.
List remaining risks.
must_not_do: claim Product Truth from visuals alone

cases.input: A Recora hero has a huge Japanese H1, CTA below the fold, and the product mock is pushed down.
expected_behavior: Identify Hero Poster Syndrome.
Reference recora-before-after-casebook Case 2.
Return H1 and first viewport acceptance criteria.

cases.input: The Product Mock is a set of random cards with no sidebar, filter, KPI hierarchy, table, source panel, or status strip.
expected_behavior: Identify Dashboard Card Pile.
Reference casebook Product Mock card pile case.
Recommend Product Mock density rebuild.

cases.input: A giant SAMPLE overlay covers the Recora dashboard mock and makes it look unfinished.
expected_behavior: Identify Sample Disclaimer Dominance.
Move sample label to toolbar/scope/caption.
Keep Product Truth disclosure visible.

cases.input: Feature Cards are only headlines and paragraphs with no mini UI.
expected_behavior: Identify Text-Only Feature Card.
Recommend one mini UI per card.
Use acceptance criteria for Feature Cards.

cases.input: The LP uses green on most cards, tabs, badges, background bands, and buttons, so every element feels primary.
expected_behavior: Identify Over-Green Interface.
Recommend deep green only for primary/active states.
Provide Tailwind color direction.

cases.input: The final CTA has a pale outline button, no product echo, and three equal actions.
expected_behavior: Identify CTA Without Gravity.
Recommend one primary action and product echo.
Use Final CTA acceptance criteria.

cases.input: On 375px mobile, the desktop mock is scaled down until its text and chart labels are unreadable.
expected_behavior: Identify Mobile Mock Shrinkage.
Recommend mobile excerpt strategy.
Use Mobile Product Mock acceptance criteria.

cases.input: Generate an implementation prompt to fix Hero Poster Syndrome and Weak Product Mock for Recora LP.
expected_behavior: Use golden-codex-prompts.
Include concrete target file, Product Truth check, commit/push ban, git remote ban, no external asset copying, validation commands, and acceptance criteria.

cases.input: Review a weak Recora LP screenshot and provide priorities.
expected_behavior: Use review-output-template.
Include Similar past failure, Golden pattern to use, Acceptance criteria, Before/after expectation, and Regression risks.

cases.input: The user says Recora still does not feel close to SE Ranking after changing layout and colors.
expected_behavior: Reference the left huge copy + right vertical mock case.
Explain the structural failure.
Select golden prompt and acceptance criteria.
